skill.name: email-handler
skill.description: Create React Email templates and integrate transactional email sending in application routes or server actions.
skill.summary: Build styled transactional emails with React Email and a shared sending helper.
skill.author: AayushBaniya2006

security_audit.summary: The external command and weak cryptography detections are false positives caused by markdown code fences, inline paths, and .tsx filenames. The skill does contain a legitimate outbound email sending workflow, so it should be published with a network-side-effect warning.

medium_findings.title: Outbound Email Sending Capability
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to render email HTML and call sendMail. This is expected for the skill, but it can send messages to external recipients if used without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sending workflow explicitly calls sendMail after rendering a template. The capability is legitimate, but it creates a real external network side effect.

low_findings.title: Public Environment Variable Reference
low_findings.description: The reference recommends process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL for absolute links. This uses environment access, but the named variable is public-facing and not a secret.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact variable name is visible and uses the NEXT_PUBLIC prefix. No evidence found of reading .env files or private credentials.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported backtick execution patterns are markdown inline code, code fences, file paths, imports, and TypeScript examples. No executable shell command or Ruby backtick execution appears in the skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows documentation examples only. The skill declares Read, Write, and Edit tools, and no shell execution instruction is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography hits appear to come from text such as markdown filenames and .tsx paths. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic algorithm use was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain a description and a .tsx creation step, not cryptographic code. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar algorithm use.

dangerous_patterns.title: Email Dispatch With Variable Recipient
dangerous_patterns.description: The reference pattern sends rendered HTML with sendMail(to, subject, html). Callers should validate recipients, subjects, and dynamic template data before dispatch.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is explicitly documented and uses variable parameters. It is not malicious, but misuse could cause spam, data leakage, or unintended notifications.

content.value_statement: Transactional email work is slow when templates, layout, and sending code drift apart. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create React Email templates and connect them to sendMail.
actual_capabilities: Creates React Email templates in src/emails.
Uses the shared Layout component with preview text.
Defines typed props for dynamic email content.
Renders templates to HTML with React Email.
Adds sendMail integration in routes or server actions.
Applies existing app configuration and theme classes.
limitations: Does not provide an email provider account or SMTP setup.
Does not verify deliverability, SPF, DKIM, or DMARC records.
Does not include recipient consent or unsubscribe logic.
Requires human review before sending production emails.

use_cases.title: Add Onboarding Emails
use_cases.description: Create welcome or setup emails that match the app layout and brand settings.

use_cases.title: Ship Account Notifications
use_cases.description: Build password reset, action required, and status update emails with typed template props.

use_cases.title: Standardize Email Patterns
use_cases.description: Keep templates, preview text, layout usage, and sendMail calls consistent across a codebase.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Email
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a welcome email template in src/emails using the shared Layout component and typed props.

prompt_templates.title: Add Sending Logic
prompt_templates.prompt: Render the new email with React Email and add a sendMail call in the relevant server action.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Existing Email
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this email template and update it to use Layout, preview text, typed props, and appConfig.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Notification Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create the email template and integration plan for a multi-step account notification flow.

output_examples.output: A typed React Email template with preview text, branded layout, reset button, and dynamic expiration copy.

output_examples.input: Send a billing alert after an invoice fails.
output_examples.output: A server-side integration that renders the billing alert template and passes recipient, subject, and HTML to sendMail.
An updated template that uses the shared Layout, app configuration, theme classes, and clear dynamic props.

best_practices: Review recipients and dynamic fields before enabling production sending.
Use absolute links from approved configuration values.
Send test emails before connecting new flows to live events.
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode secrets or provider credentials in templates.
Do not send emails from user input without validation.
Do not bypass the shared Layout for transactional templates.

faq.question: Does this skill send emails by itself?
faq.answer: No. It guides template creation and sendMail integration inside your application.

faq.question: Which email framework does it use?
faq.answer: It uses React Email components and renders templates to HTML.

faq.question: Can it create branded templates?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses the shared Layout component and appConfig values when available.

faq.question: Does it manage email provider credentials?
faq.answer: No. Provider credentials and delivery configuration remain outside the skill.

faq.question: Is this safe for production workflows?
faq.answer: Use it with review. Outbound email can notify real recipients and expose dynamic content.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
